title: Seer Prediction Specification (Lock A)
description: The forecastable object definition. Captures system, target, horizon, resolution criteria, and baseline before any analysis runs. This is Lock A — once hashed, nothing here changes.

system.description: The bounded organized system under analysis. Must pass the admissibility gate: bounded, organized, undergoing consequential change, with observable signals.

scope_notes.description: Clarifications on system boundaries agreed during scope negotiation. E.g., 'Tesla the automaker, excluding Tesla Energy and SpaceX-adjacent ventures.'

object_type.description: Classification of the system. Sets defaults for evidence sources, horizon suggestions, and monitoring logic.

target_class.description: The shape of the forecast question.

target.description: The specific forecast target statement. Must be precise enough to resolve. E.g., 'Tesla's market position relative to BYD in the global EV market over 18 months.'

length.description: Human-readable horizon length. E.g., '18 months', '3 years'.

start_date.description: ISO 8601 date. When the forecast window opens.

end_date.description: ISO 8601 date. When the forecast resolves.

criterion.description: What is being measured. E.g., 'Global EV market share relative to BYD'.

threshold.description: The specific threshold or condition for resolution. E.g., 'Tesla market share drops below BYD by >5 percentage points'.

weight.description: Component weight in the aggregate score. All weights must sum to 1.0.

source.description: The strongest-available conventional forecast Seer is competing against. E.g., 'Wall Street consensus (Bloomberg composite)'.

divergence.description: Where and why Seer's structural analysis diverges from the baseline.

hinge.description: The specific structural factor that explains the divergence. This is where Genesis earns its differential accuracy.

shock_sensitivity.description: How vulnerable the forecast is to exogenous shocks that the structural analysis cannot anticipate.

forecast_class.description: Standard: evidence supports structural diagnosis. Speculative: sparse evidence, novel domain, or untested target class.

evidence_sufficiency.description: Assessment of available evidence density at specification time.

publication_class.description: Determines public weight and calibration treatment. Research-grade carries highest trust. Exploratory excluded from primary calibration.

prediction_family.description: If this forecast belongs to a family sharing a research pass, the family ID.
